author: Ameen Hassan
summary: The user requested a full agent map across all projects in their AgentOS system. The assistant searched for the project registry, located it at /Users/ameenhassan/Desktop/Tech & Coding/Coding_projects/ameen-context-mcp/data/projects/registry.json, and read its contents to identify 7 registered projects: agentos, rfp-project, rfp_intelligence_pro, RFP_Atharii, and others. The assistant also listed the ameen-context-mcp directory structure and the broader Coding_projects directory. Using the registry data and CLAUDE.md context about Ameen's roles and projects, the assistant produced a structured agent map organized per project with role descriptions in table format. The agentos project was identified as the infrastructure layer whose agents run across all other projects. The session classifier and other core AgentOS agents were listed under the agentos project.
problems: No prior comprehensive agent map existed across all AgentOS projects
Local memory directory read failed with exit code 1 when attempting to access session memory files
approaches: Searched filesystem for registry.json to locate the project registry
Read registry.json to enumerate all 7 registered projects and their metadata
Listed ameen-context-mcp directory structure to understand available infrastructure
Listed Coding_projects directory to confirm all active projects
Synthesized agent map using registry data combined with CLAUDE.md project context
results: 7 registered projects identified: agentos, rfp-project, rfp_intelligence_pro, RFP_Atharii, and others
Full agent map produced across all projects with roles in table format
agentos confirmed as infrastructure layer running agents across all other projects
ameen-context-mcp structure confirmed: README.md, data/, hooks/, install_hooks.sh, pyproject.toml, server.py

decisions.decision: Agent map should be organized per project with role descriptions in table format
agentos is the infrastructure layer whose agents run across all other projects

open_questions: Which specific agents in the map need to be built vs. already exist?
What is the priority order for implementing agents across the 7 projects?
Are there agents needed for the behavioral health / grant work projects not yet in the registry?
